name: GitHub
one_liner: Git hosting platform for code, issues, pull requests, and CI/CD workflows.
self_serve_notes: Any developer can create a free personal access token (fine-grained or classic) or register an OAuth/GitHub App immediately, no approval needed.

api_surface.notes: Covers repos, issues, PRs, actions, packages, security alerts, orgs, and more across hundreds of endpoints plus a full GraphQL v4 API.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a GitHub toolkit with ~893 tools; GitHub also ships its own official remote/local MCP server (github/github-mcp-server).

name: Vercel
one_liner: Frontend cloud platform for deploying, hosting, and scaling web apps.
auth_methods: Other: OAuth2 (Sign in with Vercel / integrations)
self_serve_notes: Developers create personal Access Tokens directly from account settings on the free Hobby plan with no approval step.

api_surface.notes: REST API (api.vercel.com) covers deployments, projects, domains, env vars, teams, edge config, and firewall/WAF functionality.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Vercel toolkit reportedly exposes 170+ tools; no clearly documented first-party Vercel MCP server was confirmed in official docs.

name: Netlify
one_liner: Platform for building, deploying, and hosting static sites and serverless functions.
self_serve_notes: Personal access tokens are generated free and instantly from account settings on Netlify's free tier.

api_surface.notes: Covers sites, deploys, env vars, forms, DNS/SSL, webhooks, and serverless functions, documented via an OpenAPI spec.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Netlify integration page lists 0 tools/triggers despite auth methods being defined, effectively meaning no functioning toolkit exists yet; no official Netlify MCP server found.

main_blocker: No functioning Composio toolkit yet; would need to build directly against Netlify's REST API.

name: Cloudflare
one_liner: Global edge network for DNS, CDN, security (WAF), and serverless compute (Workers).
self_serve_notes: Developers self-serve API tokens (recommended) or legacy API keys directly from the dashboard on the free plan.

api_surface.notes: Over 2,500 endpoints spanning DNS, Workers, R2, KV, Zero Trust, WAF, load balancing, and more.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio offers Cloudflare toolkits; Cloudflare also ships official remote MCP servers covering Workers, R2, KV, D1, DNS, and the full API.

name: Supabase
one_liner: Open-source Firebase alternative: Postgres database, auth, storage, and edge functions.
self_serve_notes: Personal access tokens for the Management API are self-generated from the free-tier dashboard/account page.

api_surface.notes: Management API covers projects, API keys, auth providers, database webhooks, branching, and more; separate per-project REST/GraphQL data APIs also exist.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Supabase toolkit exposes 84+ tools; Supabase also maintains its own official MCP server.

name: Neo4j
one_liner: Native graph database platform for storing and querying connected data.
self_serve_notes: Neo4j Aura offers a free tier; Aura API credentials (OAuth client id/secret) can be created self-serve from the Aura console on any tier including free.

api_surface.notes: Aura API mainly manages instance lifecycle (create/pause/resume/delete databases); actual data access is via Cypher/Bolt protocol, not a broad REST surface.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio lists a Neo4j toolkit (~210 actions); Neo4j also ships an official MCP server plus community neo4j-contrib/mcp-neo4j, supporting Aura, self-managed, and Docker deployments.

name: Snowflake
one_liner: Cloud data warehouse for SQL analytics, data sharing, and AI/Cortex workloads.
auth_methods: Other: key-pair (JWT) and workload identity federation
self_serve_notes: A no-credit-card 30-day trial with $400 credits lets a developer self-serve an account and generate OAuth/key-pair credentials, though production use requires converting to paid.

api_surface.notes: SQL API for executing statements plus separate REST APIs for account/object management, Cortex AI agents, and more.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio has snowflake toolkits (Basic Auth, 15 tools); Snowflake also runs an official managed MCP server plus open-source Snowflake-Labs/mcp.

name: MongoDB Atlas
auth_methods: Other: HTTP Digest auth (public/private API keys)
self_serve_notes: Atlas free tier (M0 clusters) lets developers self-generate Administration API keys from the dashboard with no sales contact.

api_surface.notes: Atlas Administration API covers projects, clusters, users, network access, backups, and more per its full OpenAPI spec.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: No dedicated Composio 'MongoDB Atlas' toolkit was confirmed; MongoDB itself ships an official mongodb-js/mongodb-mcp-server supporting Atlas.

main_blocker: No confirmed dedicated Composio toolkit for Atlas admin API; would need to build directly against MongoDB's REST API or use its official MCP server instead.

name: Datadog
one_liner: Cloud observability platform unifying metrics, logs, traces, and security monitoring.
auth_methods: Other: Application Key (paired with API key)
self_serve_notes: A self-serve 14-day free trial lets a developer generate API/App keys immediately, but full ongoing use requires a paid plan afterward.

api_surface.notes: Covers dashboards, monitors, SLOs, hosts, downtimes, synthetics, logs, and more with 40+ tool categories in Composio's mapping.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Datadog toolkit has 40+ tools; Datadog also runs an official managed remote MCP server plus open-source datadog-labs/mcp-server.

main_blocker: Full platform access requires converting the 14-day trial to a paid plan.

name: Sentry
one_liner: Application error tracking and performance monitoring platform for developers.
self_serve_notes: Personal auth tokens are created free and instantly under User Settings on Sentry's free Developer tier.

api_surface.notes: 20+ endpoint categories covering issues, events, releases, dashboards, monitors/cron, teams, and SCIM provisioning.

existing_mcp_or_composio_toolkit.notes: Composio's Sentry toolkit has 200+ tools (OAuth-based); Sentry also runs its own official remote MCP server (mcp.sentry.dev) with OAuth, no manual API key setup needed.
